mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is an open-source cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes, enabling seamless integration of Ceph storage with Kubernetes. It automates the deployment and management of Ceph, providing self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and scalability of storage solutions.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Rook to manage Ceph resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Rook.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates the namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> This command creates a Ceph cluster.

steps.title: Verify Ceph Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-system namespace to verify the operator and cluster pods are running.
> Executes a command in the Ceph tools pod to check the cluster status.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ceph data and resources managed by Rook.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data and check the current cluster status. This step upgrades the Rook operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system get pod

```

> Check the current status of the Ceph cluster before upgrading.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status
helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create pool'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ceph cluster is not properly initialized or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Ceph cluster is healthy and that there are enough resources allocated to the nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there is a misconfiguration in the Ceph cluster or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify the configuration and permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues in the cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph health detail' to get more information on the warnings.

troubleshooting.title: Rook operator pod is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to missing CRDs or incorrect Helm chart version.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs and ensure you are using the correct Helm chart version.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for Ceph's new features, including enhanced monitoring capabilities and better resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to review the new resource requirements and adjust your cluster configuration accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-system exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
